segments.text: on the twenty-first day of the seventh month, the word of the Lord came through the prophet Haggai:
Speak to Zerubbabel son of Shealtiel, governor of Judah, to Joshua son of Jozadak, the high priest, and to the remnant of the people. Ask them,
Who of you is left who saw this house in its former glory? How does it look to you now? Does it not seem to you like nothing?
segments.speaker: Haggai

segments.text: Who of you is left who saw this house in its former glory? How does it look to you now? Does it not seem to you like nothing?
segments.speaker: Haggai

segments.text: But now be strong, Zerubbabel. Be strong, Joshua son of Jozadak, the high priest. Be strong, all you people of the land, and work. For I am with you.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: This is what I covenanted with you when you came out of Egypt. And my Spirit remains among you. Do not fear.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: In a little while I will once more shake the heavens and the earth, the sea and the dry land.
segments.speaker: The Lord Almighty

segments.text: I will shake all nations, and what is desired by all nations will come, and I will fill this house with glory.
segments.speaker: The Lord Almighty

segments.text: The silver is mine and the gold is mine.
segments.speaker: The Lord Almighty

segments.text: The glory of this present house will be greater than the glory of the former house. And in this place I will grant peace.
segments.speaker: The Lord Almighty

segments.text: On the twenty-fourth day of the ninth month, in the second year of Darius, the word of the Lord came to the prophet Haggai:
Ask the priests what the law says:
segments.speaker: The Lord Almighty

segments.text: If someone carries consecrated meat in the fold of their garment, and that fold touches some bread or stew, some wine, olive oil or other food, does it become consecrated?
No.
segments.speaker: The Priests

segments.text: Then Haggai said,
If a person defiled by contact with a dead body touches one of these things, does it become defiled?
segments.speaker: Haggai

segments.text: Yes, it becomes defiled.
segments.speaker: The Priests

segments.text: So it is with this people and this nation in my sight. Whatever they do and whatever they offer there is defiled.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: Now give careful thought to this from this day on —consider how things were before one stone was laid on another in the Lord's temple.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: When anyone came to a heap of twenty measures, there were only ten. When anyone went to a wine vat to draw fifty measures, there were only twenty.
I struck all the work of your hands with blight, mildew and hail, yet you did not return to me.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: From this day on, from this twenty-fourth day of the ninth month, give careful thought to the day when the foundation of the Lord's temple was laid. Give careful thought:
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: Is there yet any seed left in the barn? Until now, the vine and the fig tree, the pomegranate and the olive tree have not borne fruit.
From this day on I will bless you.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: The word of the Lord came to Haggai a second time on the twenty-fourth day of the month:
Tell Zerubbabel governor of Judah that I am going to shake the heavens and the earth.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: I will overturn royal thrones and shatter the power of the foreign kingdoms. I will overthrow chariots and their drivers; horses and their riders will fall, each by the sword of his brother.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: On that day, I will take you, my servant Zerubbabel son of Shealtiel, and I will make you like my signet ring, for I have chosen you.
segments.speaker: The Lord Almighty
